<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Reaper's Grasp](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4288)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 165

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=518)_ has an ashwood stock plated with worked silver, featuring a mosaic of agonized skulls carved into the metal. The mere mention of Galt’s infamous [_final blades_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094) is enough to make any mortal creature shudder. Used to execute political opponents in the nation’s constant civil war, these guillotines hold the souls of those they execute, preventing them from reaching a natural afterlife. When one of the _final blades_ was destroyed in 4710, some of the metal from its remains made its way to Alkenstar and was reforged into a deadly firearm that retained the guillotine’s soul-stealing properties.

Whenever the _reaper’s grasp_ kills a sapient living or undead creature, a portion of that creature’s soul is drawn inexorably into the weapon, and another skull is added to the mosaic as if scrawled by an invisible hand, to a maximum of 10 souls. Creatures whose souls are protected by outside forces, such as a [lich’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=438) soul cage, are immune to this effect, though the process, while painful, does not prevent the soul from going to the afterlife or being resurrected Keep track of the level of each creature whose souls are stored in the _reaper’s grasp_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64))   **Requirements** There’s at least one soul stored in the _reaper’s grasp_; **Effect** You channel the necromantic energy of a soul stored in the weapon into your next attack, increasing the attack’s destructive power. Choose a soul stored in the weapon. The next Strike you make with the _reaper’s grasp_ before the end of your turn deals an additional negative damage equal to twice the level of the creature whose soul you chose. Using this ability releases the chosen soul, and a skull on the mosaic fades away.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64))   **Requirements** There’s at least one soul stored in the _reaper’s grasp_; **Effect** You channel the necromantic energy of a soul stored in the weapon into a mantle around you, protecting you from harm. Choose a soul stored in the weapon. You gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to three times the level of the creature whose soul you chose, which last until the end of your next turn. Using this ability releases the chosen soul, and a skull on the mosaic fades away.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Reaper's Grasp

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rowan Rifle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4289)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 166

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

In a dense forest within Arcadia, djezet fell from the sky millennia ago, even before Earthfall. While this metal was poisonous to most of the plant life growing nearby, one stubborn rowan tree refused to die, its roots slowly absorbing djezet in small particles from the ground until the entire plant was suffused with it. [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) bowyers discovered the remarkable plant and coaxed it into growing into a very particular shape, its branching trunks woven together into a tightly twisted and naturally rifled barrel.

One of the legendary star guns, Arcadia’s greatest treasures, the _Rowan Rifle_ is a _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater fearsome ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2837) [speed ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2844) greater striking advanced firearm_ with a range of increment of 300 feet. It deals 1d8 fire damage and has the [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), and [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597) traits.

As a star gun, the Rowan Rifle runs on magic, and doesn’t use ammunition or black powder. As a weapon forged to protect the fey peoples of Arcadia, the _Rowan Rifle’s_ enchantments prevent it from ever causing harm to a creature with the fey trait; any attempt to shoot a fey with it results in an automatic misfire. This legendary star gun is as much a badge of office as a weapon, as it denotes dominion over all Briarbough. If the Rowan Rifle’s wielder is fey, or if they were specifically given the _Rowan Rifle_ by a fey creature who recognized them as a worthy champion, they can use the following activations.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You will the _Rowan Rifle_ to change the energy it fires from the white-hot energy of summer that glows like a star to the shivering chill of winter, the crackling electricity of the storm, or even the sonic vibrations of a roar. The _Rowan Rifle’s_ damage type changes from its current damage type to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697). On the next sunrise, the _Rowan Rifle_ returns to dealing fire damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** The _Rowan Rifle_ fires a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of liquid djezet that wraps around all creatures in the affected area before hardening, which impedes them with metallic vines unless the affected creature succeeds at a DC 35 Reflex save. A creature who fails the save takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds, and a creature who critically fails is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). A creature who succeeds at a DC 35 [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) check ends this effect. Otherwise, the djezet vines last for 10 minutes before crumbling away.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> command, envision, Interact (1 minute);  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You hold aloft the _Rowan Rifle_ and step into a tree in Briarbough, and you tree stride to another tree in Briarbough.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Rowan Rifle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tiger's Claw](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4290)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 166

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dueling Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=520)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [fearsome ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2837) [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=520)_ is made from fine tigerwood, with the head of a tiger as the muzzle. Beneath the tiger head is a claw shaped bayonet. One of a set of four guns crafted as a gift to a Zenj family for delivery of rare healing and diseaseabating herbs during an outbreak of a deadly disease in the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, these firearms are now passed down to those who have done brave acts in service to the Zenj people. The flintlock sparks thrown by this weapon take the shape of pouncing tigers and the firearm’s report sounds like a tiger’s growl. Clever wielders use the firearm’s report to panic their prey into mistakes and then pounce for the kill.

This firearm’s bullets deal slashing damage instead of piercing and add an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a critical hit. This persistent bleed damage causes tiger‑claw-shaped wounds to appear on the target.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You capitalize on the fears your firearm engenders, terrifying your foes. Make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with this firearm against a target. If you successfully deal damage to your target, the target is also affected by a 4th-level [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) with a spell DC of 28. While _phantasmal killer_ typically takes the shape of the target’s worst fear, this effect always appears to the target in the form of a majestic and ferocious tiger.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tiger's Claw

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 167 **Price** 3,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Flintlock Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=522) --- This _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [flintlock pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=522)_ bears strange, jagged markings of erratic design and has an oddly squishy grip. It can be activated to produce a variety of unusual effects. **Activate** command, Interact; **Effect** Choose a creature within 60 feet and roll a percentile die on the table below to determine the pistol’s effect. If an entry lists a spell, the pistol Casts that Spell at the indicated level (or at its lowest level, if no level is listed). You make any decisions for a spell cast by the pistol unless otherwise indicated, except that it must only target the creature you chose, or the creature you chose must be the center of the spell’s area, if it has an area but no targets. The only exception to the limitation on targeting is if the effect specifically states it targets you. If the spell’s range is less than 60 feet, increase the range to 60 feet. Any spell DC required is DC 29, and any spell attack roll required is +21. If the pistol casts a spell on you, you don’t get a saving throw or any other defense against it. Once activated, the pistol can’t be activated again for 1d4 hours. ## Pistol of Wonder
**d%****Wondrous Effect**
1–2The pistol casts [_phantom steed_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1594) (4th level) to produce a pale horse adjacent to you; only you can ride it.
3The target becomes a shiny metal color (bronze, copper, or iron) and any sound or speech becomes tinny and robotic in nature; this effect lasts 1 month.
4–5 [_The pistol casts dimensional anchor._](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)
6[ <%SPELLS%1655%%> _Modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) (6th level) causes the target to forget you ever existed; you can’t [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Spell.
7–13A short rod protrudes from the pistol and unfurls a small flag that reads “Bang!”. The pistol can't be fired until the flag is removed, which requires a single Interact action.
14–16You are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 30 feet directly away from the target; if you hit an object or creature, you stop, but take falling damage equal to the distance you moved (if you hit a creature, it takes the same amount of falling damage).
17–19The target is pushed 30 feet directly away from you; if it hits an object or creature, it stops, but takes falling damage equal to the distance it moved (if it hits another creature, the other creature takes the same amount of falling damage).
20You don't need to eat or drink for 1 week.
21–25 The pistol casts [_cloudkill_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722).
26–30The pistol casts [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=149) on you.
31The pistol casts [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) on you, except you appear as a Medium wooden grave marker bearing your name and the current date; you can’t Dismiss the spell for 1 round.
32The pistol casts [_mask of terror_](/Spells.aspx?ID=185), except the target appears as a more fearsome and violent version of itself to all observers.
33–35The target knows you have a bullet with its name on it; the first time you Strike the target with the pistol before the end of your next turn, roll the attack roll twice and take the highest result (this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect).
36–37The pistol casts [_cloak of colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=41) on you with a duration of 1d10 rounds.
38–47The pistol casts [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=154) (4th level) as water streams from the pistol.
48–50The pistol casts [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) on you, teleporting you to the space adjacent to the target opposite your current position; if that space isn’t clear, you instead teleport to the nearest open space.
51The pistol casts [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76).
52Dozens of tattered, nonmagical playing cards burst from the pistol's barrel.
53–54Shadows crowd around the target, making all creatures [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the target while not in bright light; this effect lasts for 1 hour.
55–57Normal vegetation within 30 feet of the target immediately dies and turns to ash.
58The pistol casts [_dinosaur form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=72) on you to transform you into a triceratops; the pistol protrudes from the base of your front horn, and you can fire (but not reload) the pistol while in this form.
59All non-artifact ammunition in your possession crumbles to dust.
60–69You are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 minute. You can use the extra action only to reload or fire the pistol.
70–71The target is quickened for 1 minute. It can use the extra action only to Step or Stride.
72-79The pistol casts [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742).
80–81Dozens of bullet holes appear in the target's nonmagical clothing. The effect is purely cosmetic.
82–85The pistol casts [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=211).
86The pistol vanishes, reappearing among your possessions once it can be activated again.
87–91The pistol casts [_solid fog_](/Spells.aspx?ID=290), but with swirling dust and sand instead of fog.
92–96The pistol casts [_stoneskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) on you, making your skin rough and leathery; the target ignores the [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) you gain from this spell, and the target’s attacks don’t reduce the spell’s duration.
97–99The pistol casts [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) with a duration of 3 rounds, even on a failure or critical failure.
100Reroll two results and apply both in the order rolled; further results of 100 on these rerolls have no effect.
name: Pistol of Wonder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aromatic Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4292)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The components of this ammunition emit a strong smell when combined during its activation. A creature hit by an activated aromatic ammunition gains a distinct odor for up to 1 hour or until the scent is washed off (requiring at least a gallon of water and 10 minutes of scrubbing). Any creatures within 30 feet smell the target, allowing even those with a weak sense of smell to detect its presence, and all creatures gain a +1 item bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the affected creature for as long as it has the odor. A creature that has imprecise or precise scent doubles the range at which they can detect the target using their scent compared to the normal range of their scent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aromatic Ammunition

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Awakened Metal Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4293)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These bullets are formed from a liquefied high-grade precious metal and enchanted to unlock that metal’s true potential. Each version has a different special effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 3,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a [high-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) bullet. The _awakened adamantine_ breaks through any resistance. The shot ignores the first 20 [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) a creature has to physical damage, all damage, or piercing or bludgeoning damage. Against a golem or creature who already has a resistance bypassed by adamantine, or a weakness to adamantine, the shot shatters the target’s defenses, causing them to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute instead. Because adamantine is uncommon, this version of awakened metal shot is uncommon even for gunslingers or other characters with access to uncommon guns and bullets from this book.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Cold Iron Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a [high-grade cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) bullet. The awakened cold iron attempts to disrupt enchantments the target placed on others’ minds. You can name a creature you believe to be enchanted by your target or allow the shot to choose an enchanted creature randomly. On a hit, the shot attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the enchantment the target is using to [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) that creature’s mind. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don’t critically fail) the counteract check against a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) or [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), you get a success instead. If you hit a demon or fey with no active enchantment effects on other creatures, the target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Silver Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade silver bullet. The awakened silver gleams with altered celestial moonlight designed to weaken and expose [devils](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) and [werecreatures](/Traits.aspx?ID=733). On a hit, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect on the target. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don’t critically fail) the counteract check against a devil or a werecreature, you get a success instead. If you counteracted the Change Shape ability, the creature can’t use that ability again for 1 round, or 1 minute on a critical success. If you hit a devil or werecreature not under a polymorph effect, the target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute instead.
name: Awakened Metal Shot

</column>

---

These bullets are formed from a liquefied high-grade precious metal and enchanted to unlock that metal’s true potential. Each version has a different special effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 3,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a [high-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) bullet. The _awakened adamantine_ breaks through any resistance. The shot ignores the first 20 [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) a creature has to physical damage, all damage, or piercing or bludgeoning damage. Against a golem or creature who already has a resistance bypassed by adamantine, or a weakness to adamantine, the shot shatters the target’s defenses, causing them to become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute instead. Because adamantine is uncommon, this version of awakened metal shot is uncommon even for gunslingers or other characters with access to uncommon guns and bullets from this book.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Cold Iron Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a [high-grade cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) bullet. The awakened cold iron attempts to disrupt enchantments the target placed on others’ minds. You can name a creature you believe to be enchanted by your target or allow the shot to choose an enchanted creature randomly. On a hit, the shot attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the enchantment the target is using to [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) that creature’s mind. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don’t critically fail) the counteract check against a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) or [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), you get a success instead. If you hit a demon or fey with no active enchantment effects on other creatures, the target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute instead.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Silver Shot)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 168

**Price** 2,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shot is a high-grade silver bullet. The awakened silver gleams with altered celestial moonlight designed to weaken and expose [devils](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) and [werecreatures](/Traits.aspx?ID=733). On a hit, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect on the target. The counteract level is 9, and the counteract modifier is +27. If you fail (but don’t critically fail) the counteract check against a devil or a werecreature, you get a success instead. If you counteracted the Change Shape ability, the creature can’t use that ability again for 1 round, or 1 minute on a critical success. If you hit a devil or werecreature not under a polymorph effect, the target is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute instead.
name: Awakened Metal Shot (Awakened Adamantine Shot)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Powder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4294)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Black powder is a volatile and explosive alchemical substance commonly used in the production of firearm munitions. Black powder becomes inert and useless when wet and must be kept in a sealed, water-tight container.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Dose or Round)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 1 cp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The smallest unit of black powder that still has a simple use, a dose can be a simple package paper parcel around black powder or it can be packaged with a metal bullet or pellet to be used as ammunition. When ignited with a fuse or exposed to direct flame, a dose of black powder explodes. This isn’t powerful enough to deal damage but makes a loud sound and could trigger further explosions. A fuse for a dose can be created with a few twists of paper and causes the dose to explode the round after it’s lit.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Horn)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A horn contains 500 doses worth of black powder. A horn can be detonated by leaving a small trail of black powder and then lighting it. Each 5-foot square requires one dose of black powder to create a trail through. As long as the horn is mostly full (at least 400 doses remaining) it can be detonated to deal 1d6 fire damage in a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). It takes 1 round per 15 feet of powder trail laid down for a horn to detonate after the trail is lit. If multiple horns detonate simultaneously, it can increase the area of the explosion, but the damage in overlapping areas doesn’t increase.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Keg)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A keg contains 5,000 doses of black powder and can be detonated in the same way as a horn. As long as the keg is mostly full (at least 4,000 doses remaining) this deals 3d6 fire damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Detonating multiple kegs can increase the area, but not the damage, of this effect; detonating a keg and any horns at the same time in an overlapping area also doesn’t increase the damage.
name: Black Powder

---

A keg contains 5,000 doses of black powder and can be detonated in the same way as a horn. As long as the keg is mostly full (at least 4,000 doses remaining) this deals 3d6 fire damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Detonating multiple kegs can increase the area, but not the damage, of this effect; detonating a keg and any horns at the same time in an overlapping area also doesn’t increase the damage.
name: Black Powder (Horn)

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Black powder is a volatile and explosive alchemical substance commonly used in the production of firearm munitions. Black powder becomes inert and useless when wet and must be kept in a sealed, water-tight container.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Dose or Round)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 1 cp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The smallest unit of black powder that still has a simple use, a dose can be a simple package paper parcel around black powder or it can be packaged with a metal bullet or pellet to be used as ammunition. When ignited with a fuse or exposed to direct flame, a dose of black powder explodes. This isn’t powerful enough to deal damage but makes a loud sound and could trigger further explosions. A fuse for a dose can be created with a few twists of paper and causes the dose to explode the round after it’s lit.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Black Powder (Horn)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A horn contains 500 doses worth of black powder. A horn can be detonated by leaving a small trail of black powder and then lighting it. Each 5-foot square requires one dose of black powder to create a trail through. As long as the horn is mostly full (at least 400 doses remaining) it can be detonated to deal 1d6 fire damage in a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). It takes 1 round per 15 feet of powder trail laid down for a horn to detonate after the trail is lit. If multiple horns detonate simultaneously, it can increase the area of the explosion, but the damage in overlapping areas doesn’t increase.

level="2"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A keg contains 5,000 doses of black powder and can be detonated in the same way as a horn. As long as the keg is mostly full (at least 4,000 doses remaining) this deals 3d6 fire damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Detonating multiple kegs can increase the area, but not the damage, of this effect; detonating a keg and any horns at the same time in an overlapping area also doesn’t increase the damage.
name: Black Powder (Keg)

markdown:
**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 169 **Price** 900 gp **Ammunition** round **Activate**command --- The night sky seems contained in this glass projectile. When an activated _dreaming round_ damages a creature, it induces drowsiness. The creature must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature becomes fatigued and slowed 1, and must attempt another Fortitude save at the end of each of its turns. If it fails, its slowed condition increases by 1. A successful save reduces the slowed condition by 1. If the slowed condition is removed or reduced to 0, the effect and the fatigued condition end. If the target’s actions are reduced to 0 by this effect, it immediately falls into a deep sleep and is [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), during which it no longer attempts a save at the end of its turn. It wakes up automatically after 1 hour or if it takes damage, but not due to non-painful stimuli (such as noise or being nudged). When it wakes up, its slowed condition decreases by 1, though it must once again save at the end of each of its turns and might risk falling asleep again.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the target is initially slowed 2.
name: Dreaming Round

Eroding bullets cast a faint green glow, and smell like the sicklysweet organic gases that rise from corpses. Handling an eroding bullet without gloves deals 1 point of acid damage and leaves the putrid scent coated on your fingers. Upon Striking an enemy,the glass casing inside the bullet bursts, releasing a splattering of bubbling green acid that coats the target. The target takes 2d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) in addition to the damage normally dealt by the attack.

This ammunition includes a small reservoir of a tacky red substance that coats the ammunition when you activate it. The substance makes a creature bleed more freely. For 1 minute after you deal damage to a creature with an activated exsanguinating ammunition that creature gains the listed weakness to [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). In addition, the DC of any [flat checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end persistent bleed damage increases from 15 to 17 (from 10 to 12 when receiving particularly effective assistance) for the duration.

These bullets glimmer with emerald green light that dances across the surface of the bullet like a mischievous [sprite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3210). On a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), a _fairy bullet_ casts [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) (DC 23) extending outward from a corner of the target's space. You choose which corner of the target's space you want the burst to extend out from at the time you declare the associated Strike. Since the _fairy bullet_ is fired before _revealing light_ can reveal the target, the effects don’t affect the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) for the attack roll with the _fairy bullet_ if the target is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from you.

</column>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 170

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These cartridges are filled with sticky clear glue. When a glue bullet hits, a syrupy webbing coats the target and sticks to the ground or a nearby surface, hindering their movement. The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 2d4 rounds, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) against a DC of 18. On a critical hit, the target is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until it Escapes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Glue Bullet

<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This craggy stone ammunition is warm to the touch. When you fire an activated _meteor shot_, it explodes into a small swarm of meteors as it reaches its target, scorching nearby creatures and littering the ground with rubble. In addition to the weapon’s normal damage, the _meteor shot_ deals fire damage and the ground in the area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366).

In addition, the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) gains the following critical failure effect.

**Critical Failure** The weapon misfires.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stepping Stone Shot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4302)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 171

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** round

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A series of small stone discs pressed into a single round make up this ammunition. When you fire an activated _stepping stone shot_, whether you hit or miss your target, the shot creates a series of supports in a line that creatures can walk on as if solid ground. The line can ascend or descend at a 45-degree angle. The discs support any amount of weight, but don’t otherwise pose any sort of obstacle; creatures and attacks can move through them if they wish. They crumble to dust if anything attempts to move or otherwise manipulate them. A creature can use a two-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to its speed on the stones while causing them to crumble behind it. This ammunition does not work in firearms with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait. The maximum length of the line depends on the type of ammunition. However, the line also can’t extend beyond the maximum distance for a Strike from your firearm (usually six times the firearm’s range increment).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stepping Stone Shot
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 171

**Price** 65 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The stones reach up to 100 feet, and they last up to the end of your next turn if no one collapses them.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Stepping Stone Shot (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 171

**Price** 185 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The stones reach up to 250 feet, and they last up to 1 minute if no one collapses them.
name: Stepping Stone Shot

This ammunition does not work in firearms with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait. The maximum length of the line depends on the type of ammunition. However, the line also can’t extend beyond the maximum distance for a Strike from your firearm (usually six times the firearm’s range increment).

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</row>

</column>

---

A series of small stone discs pressed into a single round make up this ammunition. When you fire an activated _stepping stone shot_, whether you hit or miss your target, the shot creates a series of supports in a line that creatures can walk on as if solid ground. The line can ascend or descend at a 45-degree angle. The discs support any amount of weight, but don’t otherwise pose any sort of obstacle; creatures and attacks can move through them if they wish. They crumble to dust if anything attempts to move or otherwise manipulate them. A creature can use a two-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to its speed on the stones while causing them to crumble behind it. This ammunition does not work in firearms with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait. The maximum length of the line depends on the type of ammunition. However, the line also can’t extend beyond the maximum distance for a Strike from your firearm (usually six times the firearm’s range increment).

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Trustworthy Round](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4303)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 171

**Price** 9 gp

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** any

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>command</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ammunition was developed in Dongun Hold to minimize casualties to friendly fire, and is always marked by a burnished copper head or tip so it can be easily identified. Before you can fire a _trustworthy round_, you must call out a target. You don’t need to specify a name; the target could be “The angry tiger attacking our group on the left.” The round will only hit the specified target and will turn to gossamer dust midair if it misses the intended target or comes into contact with anything else; this also prevents abilities that redirect attacks. The round doesn’t have any capabilities beyond your own to determine whether someone is who you think they are, so you can’t use it to determine a disguised creature’s identity. If you specify a target of “Seltyiel” and shoot someone disguised as Seltyiel who you thought was Seltyiel, the attack will still hit, whereas if you were about to hit a disguised Seltyiel who you didn’t recognize to be Seltyiel, the round would dissolve.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Trustworthy Round

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sambuca](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4304)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 177

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sambuca is a long, covered troop bridge designed to help troops move from sea to land. Preparing it for use requires lashing together two [galleys](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=75) or other ships of similar size. The sambuca is then mounted across the ships on one end with its length suspended upward, and attached to pulleys fastened to the tops of the ships’ masts. Once the ships are moved into position, the sambuca can be lowered to rest on a battlement or other surface, allowing troops to rush from the ships onto their intended destination.

The sambuca has AC 17, Fort +11, Ref +5, [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 8, HP 32 (BT 16), and object immunities. It’s covered on all sides to protect those who cross, granting them greater cover. The end of the sambuca can be lowered by teams operating the pulleys in concert. This requires three crew members on each ship, and they can lower the far end of the sambuca by 15 feet each round they work.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sambuca

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camouflage Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4305)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 178

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lightweight mesh fits easily over light armor. The suit is designed to incorporate local flora and ground clutter into the mesh to help you blend in seamlessly with the environment. Due to the abrasive nature of the materials used, this item is unsuitable for use by unarmored characters. You can prepare the suit for use within your current environment by using an exploration activity that takes at least 10 minutes, but sometimes longer if the materials are hard to find or the environment is unusual enough to warrant additional difficulty in preparing camouflage that can blend with it consistently.

A suit prepared in this manner grants you a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks while you attempt to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) in the specific environment it has been prepared for The suit remains usable in this manner until you rest for the night, though it doesn’t grant the benefit whenever you aren’t in the appropriate environment. The GM might rule that some environments are unusual enough that you can’t create a camouflage suit appropriate for the environment. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Camouflage Suit
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 179

**Price** 10 gp

right="Item 9"

A superb camouflage suit is fashioned from especially well-blended materials and gives you a +2 item bonus to the check instead of a +1 item bonus.

You can insert or remove earplugs from your ears or a willing creature’s ears with a single [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action using one hand. They take a –2 circumstance penalty to all [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) Perception checks but also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against auditory effects.

Practice targets can appear in many shapes and sizes and usually come in packs of 10 held in protective cases made of heavy cloth or light leather.

[Silencer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4310)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 179

**Price** 1 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One of the more prolifically used devices developed in the infamous Alkenstar Gunworks, these small firearm components are capable of muffling most of the weapons’ explosive sound when fired. Without a silencer, a firearm’s shot makes a loud and distinctive bang, which can easily be heard through doors and thin walls, but firearms equipped with silencers only make a quiet noise when fired. Due to engineering constraints, a silencer can’t be attached to any firearm with the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) trait. Attaching a silencer to a firearm takes 1 minute, and the silencer is consumed the first time a shot is fired through it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silencer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4311)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 179

**Price** 2 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tripods are designed for use with [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) weapons, as a way for gunslingers with lower strength to accurately use these more powerful weapons by sacrificing mobility instead. They can be set up and attached to a firearm with a single Interact action using one hand, setting the tripod in your square. While this sturdy piece of engineering is in use, you don’t take the –2 penalty for firing a kickback weapon, even if your Strength isn’t high enough to avoid the penalty. However, you must retrieve the tripod with a single [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action before you can move the firearm to a different position. Normally, when you’re [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), you become observed if you do anything except [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), or [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304). However, deploying or retrieving a tripod with an Interact action doesn’t automatically make you observed, so long as you don’t set up or remove the tripod when it’s in a spot where creatures can see the tripod itself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tripod

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gunner's Bandolier](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4313)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 179

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This incredibly spacious bandolier can hold up to 4 onehanded crossbows or firearms that take no more than 1 action to completely reload (typically meaning that weapons with the capacity or repeating traits won’t fit in the bandolier’s holsters). A _gunner’s bandolier_ can be etched with runes as though it were a ranged weapon. When you invest the _gunner’s bandolier_, you can attune it to each of the 4 weapons holstered in it.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You empower one of the attuned weapons in the bandolier, granting it the runes etched onto the _gunner’s bandolier_ and removing the runes from any previously drawn weapon. Then, you [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw the weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision;     **Effect** All weapons that were attuned to the bandolier when you invested it, not including any weapons you’re currently wielding, return to the bandolier, and one of the returned weapons is automatically reloaded.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gunner's Bandolier

    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immaculate Holsters](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4314)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pristine leather belt is made of treated and polished black leather with silver fittings; it features a pair of matching leather holsters that can each fit a one-handed firearm or hand crossbow.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Up to two firearms currently holstered in the _immaculate holsters_ are instantly cleaned and oiled, protecting them from accidental misfires (though not misfires caused as a result of using a feat or ability). The holstered weapons are also reloaded with non-magical 0-level ammunition appropriate to a weapon of their type; if a firearm has multiple chambers, such as a [slide pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=524), each empty chamber is loaded. _Immaculate holsters_ can’t reload the cartridge of a [repeating](/Traits.aspx?ID=374) weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immaculate Holsters

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._ **Usage** worn **Bulk** L --- This beige bandolier has a rectangular holster, which allows it to contain a standard-sized [Harrow deck](/Equipment.aspx?ID=824), and red pockets embroidered with goldwork in a strange but appealing mixture of Alkenstar and Varisian motifs. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You draw a card from the bandolier and [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to load the card into a gun or crossbow you’re wielding that requires 1 action to reload. The drawn card immediately transforms into magical ammunition with a type depending on the drawn card’s suit, and a new copy of that card returns to the deck, ready to be drawn again. When the card transforms into ammunition, it transforms into a firearm round or crossbow bolt appropriate for the weapon you chose to reload, even if magical ammunition of the drawn variety would normally be limited to ammunition of a different type (such as a storm arrow normally only being available in arrow form). Either draw a card from the Harrow deck or roll 1d6 to determine the suit of the card. The result determines the type of magical ammunition the card becomes, as per the table below. Magical ammunition created this way lasts 10 minutes or until you fire it, whichever comes first. ## Lucky Draw Bandolier
**d6****Ability (Suit)****Ammunition**
1Strength (Hammer)[_Meteor shot_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1196)
2Dexterity (Key)[_Storm arrow_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=169)
3Constitution (Shield)[_Corrosive ammunition_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=711)
4Intelligence (Book)[_Explosive shot_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=162)
5Wisdom (Star)[_Terrifying ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=725)
6Charisma (Crown)[_Fairy bullet_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1194)
 Lucky Draw Bandolier **Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180 **Price** 700 gp Lucky Draw Bandolier (Greater) **Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180 **Price** 2,000 gp --- You can activate a _greater lucky draw bandolier_ once per hour instead of being able to activate it once per day.
name: Lucky Draw Bandolier

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Darkvision Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 180

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These scopes, popular with snipers and other sneaky sharpshooters who ply their trade in the dead of night, incorporate clouded crystals with magical properties into their design. While relatively useless under normal lighting conditions, these crystals can help bring things into focus when used in dim light. The scope is then given an enchantment to enhance these properties for use in darkness. The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) visible through the scope (as well as in areas of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404), if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Darkvision Scope
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Darkvision Scope (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

**Price** 5,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2 and activating the scope grants [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.
name: Darkvision Scope

The scope grants you a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight in areas of [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) visible through the scope (as well as in areas of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404), if the scope has been activated).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You gain [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) until the beginning of your next turn, as long as you continue to look through the scope.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetite Scope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4317)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

More of a field projector than a scope, this small cylinder of magnetite has been enchanted to spread and accelerate the shrapnel of a scatter weapon further than the weapon’s natural capabilities. Furthermore, some of the floating magnetite inside the scope’s structure can help you when attempting to determine the your allies’ locations. The magnetite scope grants you a +2 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) when using the scope to assist you in navigating. This scope can only be attached to firearms with the scatter trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** The next Strike you make this round with the gun to which the scope is attached has its scatter radius increased by 5 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Magnetite Scope
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

**Price** 800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Magnetite Scope (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 181

**Price** 16,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, and activating the scope increases the scatter radius by 10 feet.
name: Magnetite Scope

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scope of Limning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4319)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) gunsmiths of Dongun Hold originally created these scopes to help them clear out vermin in underground areas. This scope captures the sound that echoes off a creature hit by the firearm and transforms it into light, illuminating the target for all to see.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157))    **Effect** If your next [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) from the weapon to which the scope is attached hits a creature, the sound of the impact transforms into light, causing the creature to glow until the end of your next turn. A visible creature can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while they glow. If a creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), they’re concealed while glowing, rather than being undetected. Because the effect requires a solid impact, incorporeal creatures are unaffected unless the bullet can deal force damage or has the effects of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Limning

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scope of Truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4320)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Replace the word "transmutation" in the scope of truth with the word "polymorph."_

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm or crossbow (scope)

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _scope of truth_ is a bit bigger than most to accommodate larger lenses, which have been magically prepared with tiny shards from crystal balls to reveal the truth. The scope grants you a +2 item bonus to Perception checks made to Seek in areas you can see through the scope.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, you can see things through the scope as they actually are. The GM rolls a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check with a +20 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 7 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) or [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) in the area, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through it, not to end the spell or effect. For instance, if the check succeeds against a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) spell, you can see the creature’s true form, but you don’t end the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Scope of Truth

    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Gunner's Saddle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn saddle

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Developed by marauders from the Mana Wastes, this clockwork saddle comes with a retractable weapon mount that can be used as a [tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1207) to stabilize a weapon with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) trait. Just like a normal tripod, you Interact to deploy the tripod to stabilize the firearm, and then again to retract the tripod to move it. The saddle uses complex hydraulics to protect the steed from the firearm’s recoil.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gunner's Saddle

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immovable Tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4322)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This copper tripod with an [_immovable rod_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=259) at its core is a perfect example of engineering ingenuity applied to magic items, adapting the eccentric power of an _immovable rod_ for the pragmatic use of stabilizing weapons without the need for solid ground on which to stabilize them. In addition to extraplanar and aquatic environments, these devices have seen use by [flying](/Rules.aspx?ID=3374) and climbing snipers who would otherwise have no way to mitigate their weapons’ fierce kickback.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You deploy the tripod and press a button to lock it into place via the _immovable rod_, allowing you to deploy the tripod in midair, underwater, or anywhere else where you don’t have a solid horizontal surface available. If you Activate the tripod by pushing the button again, you release and retrieve the tripod. While anchored, the tripod can be moved only if 8,000 pounds of pressure are applied to it or if a creature uses [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) the tripod with a DC of 40 (though most intelligent creatures can just push the button to release the tripod).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immovable Tripod

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Portable Weapon Mount](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4323)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Powerful firearms like the arquebus were originally used to defend fortifications or ships, mounted on casements or pintles to steady their aim and offset their recoil. More mobile means of stabilizing weapons with kickback were developed as firearms began to spread across the Inner Sea. The standard tripod takes an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to deploy using one hand.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Portable Weapon Mount (Monopod)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 182

**Price** 2 sp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Monopods are lighter and can be deployed with a single hand using the same action as drawing the firearm. They still require an Interact action to retrieve. Monopods are less stable than a tripod, and firing a kickback weapon from a monopod without the necessary Strength reduces the penalty to a –1 circumstance penalty instead of removing it entirely.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Portable Weapon Mount (Tripod, Shielded)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 5

</row>

</column>

---

A shielded tripod resembles a squat shield on a tripod. You can deploy and retrieve a shielded tripod with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, as normal, but while a shielded tripod is in your square, you can use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) action to gain standard cover behind the tripod’s shield. You can’t use this cover to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), as normal for times when your cover still leaves your position obvious.
name: Portable Weapon Mount

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Air Cartridge Firing system](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4324)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons. Weapons that install an air cartridge firing system and that have the kickback trait retain the trait and the associated drawbacks._

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Air cartridge firing mechanisms use a container of compressed air affixed to a sealed system that releases the air in a small burst to propel ammunition, and they’re the default used in Arcadian [air repeaters](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188). Any firearm can be modified to replace its normal firing mechanism with an air cartridge firing system, allowing the weapon to be fired underwater or in other conditions that would normally prevent the ignition of black powder. The air cartridges lack much of the propulsive power of black powder, however, imposing a –10-foot penalty to the attached firearm’s range increment. Weapons with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) trait don’t gain that trait’s benefits when using an air cartridge firing system. Attaching an air cartridge firing system takes one hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Air Cartridge Firing system

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blazons of Shared Power](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4325)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn and attached to two weapons (see below)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass emblems come in a variety of designs, usually customized to the purchaser to reflect the heraldry of a family or guild. _Blazons of shared power_ come in sets of three. When you invest the blazons, you wear one of the three on your chest, and you attach the others to a pair of one-handed weapons, choosing one as the primary weapon and one as the secondary weapon. These weapons can be either melee weapons or ranged weapons. As long as you’re wielding both the primary weapon and the secondary weapon, the secondary weapon gains the benefit of the [fundamental](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167) runes on the primary weapon. A weapon can only have a single _blazon of shared power_ attached to it at a time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

**Price** 52 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 5

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 5

</row>

</column>

---

A set of _greater blazons of shared power_ also replicates property runes from the primary weapon, so long as the secondary weapon meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and isn’t a specific weapon. The secondary weapon gains the benefits of those runes. All of its own runes are suppressed. When you invest the blazons, you can elect for them to transfer only fundamental runes, in which case they function as standard _blazons of shared power_.
name: Blazons of Shared Power

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn and attached to two weapons (see below)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass emblems come in a variety of designs, usually customized to the purchaser to reflect the heraldry of a family or guild. _Blazons of shared power_ come in sets of three. When you invest the blazons, you wear one of the three on your chest, and you attach the others to a pair of one-handed weapons, choosing one as the primary weapon and one as the secondary weapon. These weapons can be either melee weapons or ranged weapons. As long as you’re wielding both the primary weapon and the secondary weapon, the secondary weapon gains the benefit of the [fundamental](/Rules.aspx?ID=3167) runes on the primary weapon. A weapon can only have a single _blazon of shared power_ attached to it at a time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blazons of Shared Power
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 5

</row>

</column>

---

A set of _greater blazons of shared power_ also replicates property runes from the primary weapon, so long as the secondary weapon meets all the prerequisites for a given rune and isn’t a specific weapon. The secondary weapon gains the benefits of those runes. All of its own runes are suppressed. When you invest the blazons, you can elect for them to transfer only fundamental runes, in which case they function as standard _blazons of shared power_.
name: Blazons of Shared Power (Greater)

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 183

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters with access to firearms gain access to accessories that can be used with those weapons._

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These spring-loaded inserts can be fitted into the breech of a double-barreled firearm over the course of 10 minutes or during the firearm’s daily maintenance. After the weapon is fired, the ejectors hasten the reloading process by ejecting the spent detritus from the fired rounds. This allows you to reload both barrels of the double-barreled weapon as a single Interact action the next time you reload the weapon as long as you do so before the end of your next turn. However, the ejectors are consumed in the process, and you must spend the time to insert a new set to gain the benefit again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Breech Ejectors

[Large Bore Modifications](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4327)
</title>

<traits>

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These modifications include a heavier stock and larger replacement barrel designed to increase the stopping power of firearms. Large bore modifications can only be applied to firearms with the [kickback](/Traits.aspx?ID=847) or [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=848) traits, and attaching large bore modifications takes 1 hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

When you attach large bore modifications to a kickback weapon, the bonus to damage granted by the kickback trait increases from 1 to 2 additional damage and the Strength requirement to fire the weapon without penalty increases to 18.

When you attach large bore modifications to a weapon with the scatter trait, the radius of the scatter effect increases by 5 feet, but the weapon imposes a –2 penalty on attack rolls if the wielder’s Strength score is less than 14. If the weapon has both the kickback and scatter traits, apply both sets of modifications with the Strength requirement raised to 18 to avoid penalties when firing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Large Bore Modifications

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to firearm (firing mechanism)

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This device replaces the attached firearm’s normal firing mechanism (normally, most of the guns in this chapter use a flintlock or matchlock firing mechanism). When the firearm’s wielder fires the weapon, a small rune etched on a piece of stone affixed inside the mechanism releases a magical spark that’s propelled through the firing mechanism and into the firearm, launching its bullet. An _underwater firing mechanism_ allows the attached firearm to be fired underwater or in other conditions that would normally prevent the ignition of black powder. Attaching an _underwater firing mechanism_ to a firearm takes 1 hour, though this time can overlap with the standard time required to maintain and clean your firearm to prevent misfires.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Underwater Firing Mechanism

This tiny copper gear is attached to the side of a firearm with a matching bolt or pin. When you activate it, the affixed weapon magically transfigures itself into the form of any simple or martial firearm to which you have access, harmlessly ejecting any contained ammunition in the process. Any runes or attached items present on the affixed weapon remain active unless incompatible with its new form, in which case they’re suppressed for the duration of the transformation. The effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Indomitable Keepsake](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4334)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You’re critically hit by a firearm attack.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman usually takes the form of a small sentimental object carried in a pocket or attached to the inside of a piece of armor. When you activate it, it slows the attack, and you reduce the damage from the triggering critical hit by 10, as the attack destroys the talisman. This effect only reduces the additional damage from a critical hit; it can’t reduce the damage below the amount it would deal on a normal hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Indomitable Keepsake
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Indomitable Keepsake (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate a _greater indomitable keepsake_, you reduce the damage from a firearm critical hit by 20 instead of reducing it by 10.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Indomitable Keepsake (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate a major indomitable keepsake, you reduce the damage from a firearm critical hit by 30 instead of reducing it by 10.
name: Indomitable Keepsake

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Peacemaker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4335)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision, manipulate</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** Your last action was an Interact action to stow the affixed firearm or crossbow
</row>

</column>

---

This ragged piece of white cloth is wrapped around the grip, stock, or haft of the affixed weapon. When you activate the talisman, you gain the effects of a [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) spell (DC 20) lasting for 1 minute. If you draw the affixed firearm, the effect ends immediately and the talisman crumbles.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Peacemaker
requirement_markdown: Your last action was an Interact action to stow the affixed firearm or crossbow
