<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Persistent Lodestone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4336)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm with a reload of 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You miss on a ranged Strike with the affixed weapon using an ordinary 0-level piece of ammunition.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small magnetite block is attached to the barrel of the firearm by a thin metal wire drilled through a hole in its center. When you activate the lodestone, the ammunition from your missed shot is immediately recalled to your firearm, allowing you to fire again without reloading.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Persistent Lodestone

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shrieking Skull](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4337)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm or crossbow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You’re an expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) and the affixed weapon is loaded.
</row>

</column>

---

This dried skull of a snake is mounted atop the firearm’s barrel or affixed to a crossbow’s stock. When activated, the skull crawls onto the ammunition loaded in the affixed weapon. If you Strike with the weapon before the end of your turn, the skull lets out a bloodcurdling scream as the ammunition approaches its target. Regardless of whether the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) is a success, the screaming skull allows you to attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the target as well as each enemy within 30 feet of the target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shrieking Skull
requirement_markdown: You’re an expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) and the affixed weapon is loaded.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sniper's Bead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4338)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a two-handed firearm or crossbow

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a ranged Strike with the affixed weapon before rolling

**Requirement** You’re a master with the affixed weapon.
</row>

</column>

---

This plain wooden bead dangles from a string attached to the stock of the affixed weapon. When you activate the bead, its magic greatly lessens the effect of distance on your triggering attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no range increment penalty on your attack, as long as the attack is against a target within the affixed weapon’s first two range increments.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no range increment penalty on your attack, as long as the attack is against a target within the affixed weapon’s first four range increments.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sniper's Bead (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You take no range increment penalty on your attack, even if the target is all the way out to the weapon’s sixth range increment. As normal, you still can’t hit a target more than six range increments away.
name: Sniper's Bead
requirement_markdown: You’re a master with the affixed weapon.

</row>

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silver Tripod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4339)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 185

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a firearm with the  kickback  trait

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny, silver facsimile of a weapon tripod is usually attached to the underside of the affixed weapon’s barrel. When activated, it creates an invisible construct of magical force that attaches to the weapon and automatically stabilizes it in any location, even in midair. The effect lasts for 1 minute or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it. The effect also ends immediately if you let go of the affixed weapon. The affixed weapon cannot be moved while this effect is active.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silver Tripod

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 73 **Price** 75 gp --- This snare hides a tiny humanoid figurine made of enchanted ice among leaves or just beneath a thin layer of dirt in the square. The first creature to enter the snare's square crushes the statuette, releasing an incorporeal figure of chilly mist that grabs at the triggering creature, dealing 8d8 cold damage. The creature must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 1 round as the icy figure clings to its legs.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and [1d6 persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from the icy figure's embrace. It is clumsy 1 for as long as it takes this persistent damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and 2d6 persistent cold damage from the icy figure’s embrace. It IS clumsy 2 for as long as it takes this persistent damage.
name: Freezing Embrace Snare

**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is pushed 10 feet in the determined direction.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is pushed 15 feet in the determined direction, and is drenched with water, taking a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) effects for 1 minute.

When a creature enters the snare's square, it is plunged into an unnatural darkness for 1 minute. Light can't enter the square, any non-magical light sources within the square do not emanate any light, and magical [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640) emanating from a spell of rank 2 or below is suppressed. Those outside the area can't see directly into or through the [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401), and all adjacent squares are reduced to dim light.

</column>
name: Shadow Cloak Snare

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Soul Cage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4347)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 219, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 51

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This item is crafted by a spellcaster who wishes to become a lich. When a lich is destroyed, their soul flees to the _soul cage_. The soul cage then rebuilds the lich's undead body over the course of 1d10 days. Afterward, the lich manifests next to the _soul cage_, fully healed and in a new body (therefore, they lack any equipment they had on their old body). A lich's soul cage must be destroyed to prevent a lich from returning.

 The standard _soul cage_ is a sealed metal box containing strips of parchment inscribed with magical phrases. This box has Hardness 9 and 36 HP, but some liches devise more durable or difficult-to-obtain _soul cages_. A _soul cage_ might also come in the form of a ring, an amulet, or a similar item; the specifics are up to the creator.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soul Cage

This liquid contains blood from a juvenile green dragon. For 1 hour after you drink the potion, you can breathe out a magical cloud of poison. Exhaling this dragon breath uses a single action. The cloud of poison deals 2d6 poison damage in a 15-foot cone, and each creature in the area must attempt a DC 19 basic Fortitude save. After you use the dragon breath, you can't do so again for 1d4 rounds.

>
[Pyrite Rat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4349)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Beginner Box: Game Master's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=324) pg. 54, [Secrets of the Unlit Star Game Master's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=326) pg. 62

**Price** 32 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lustrous, rat-shaped pyrite statuette is 1 inch tall. When activated, the statuette transforms into a flesh-and-blood giant rat. You can use the following action when you hold the _pyrite rat_. You can use this action only once per day, and the rat remains transformed for 1 hour.

**Activate—Transform Statue** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You place the statue on solid ground and speak the rat’s secret name, causing the statuette to transform into a living [giant rat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162). In creature form, the giant rat acts on your turn. It gets 2 actions and can’t use reactions. You have to spend an action each turn to tell it what to do; otherwise, it tries to run away from danger or cowers where it is. If the rat is slain while in animal form, it reverts to its statue shape and can’t be transformed again until 1 week has passed. If the figurine is destroyed while in its statue form, it’s shattered and its magical properties are lost forever.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pyrite Rat

The storage adjustment fits the armor or clothing with belts, buckles, pouches, and loops for holding and storing tools. Countless fasteners make the armor as jangly as [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46). While wearing armor with this adjustment, you can wear up to 3 Bulk of tools instead of the usual 2. However, the armor acquires the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=660) trait. If it already has the noisy trait, increase its penalty to Stealth checks by 1.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4359)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 11

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A suit of armor with this adjustment incorporates short, flexible harnesses meant to connect weapons to each of its vambraces. These harnesses can each be connected to a melee weapon of light Bulk or less. Attaching or removing a weapon takes an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. Someone else can attach or remove a weapon if you’re willing to let them or you’re unable to act. You must remove a weapon from its mount before you can completely [Release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) or otherwise stow it.

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) you of a weapon connected to the armor. If the weapon would be knocked from your grasp or you would drop it, the weapon dangles from the bracer by its harness rather than falling to the ground. You can regain control of the weapon in the normal time it takes you to draw it. Attaching a weapon to a weapon harness prevents you from throwing it or using other abilities that would require it to leave your person.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon Harness

This rune charges up as you defeat your foes, driving you forward across the battlefield with every victory.

This movement doesn’t trigger reactions. You can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assisting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4362)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 12

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your armor provides support for your joints or advanced prostheses for missing limbs, holding your body in place and easing physical symptoms. This replicates the benefits of any number of [splints, supports, and prostheses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2213). When you invest the armor, you determine how many such supports you want, and where on your body they assist you. 

In addition, the extra support and structure provided by the armor allows you to transport more than you would otherwise normally be able to. You can carry Bulk equal to 6 + your Strength modifier before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70), and you can hold and carry a total Bulk of up to 11 + your Strength modifier.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Assisting

---

A complex pattern of protective symbols gives protection against various forms of energy, but only part of the runic structure can be active at a given time.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You take [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage;  **Effect** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to the triggering damage type. This doesn’t apply to the triggering damage. This resistance lasts until you Activate this rune again or the armor is no longer invested by you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

The armor allows you to make a controlled descent.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** You glide slowly toward the ground, 5 feet down and up to 25 feet forward through the air. Provided you spend at least 1 action gliding on your turn and haven’t yet reached the ground, you remain in the air at the end of your turn. Otherwise, you [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune utilizes magical principles used in an [_immovable rod_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=259).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your armor anchors you in place, even defying gravity, rendering you [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until you Dismiss the Activation. While you’re immobilized in this way, you can be moved only if a creature succeeds at a DC 40 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) your armor. You can also be moved if 8,000 pounds of pressure are placed upon you, though this is likely fatal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immovable

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magnetizing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4366)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto metal armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune alters the magnetic polarity of your armor, making other metal items drawn to it ever so slightly. You can amplify the magnetic power of the armor to keep another creature from getting away from you.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** A creature adjacent to you is made of [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) or wearing metal armor; **Effect** You magnetize your armor. If you or the target attempt to move away from one another, treat each square as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). This doesn’t affect movement that keeps you the same distance from one another, so you could still Step if you remained adjacent to the target. This effect ends once either of you is no longer adjacent to the other at the end of an action. 

This Activation might work on some creatures partially made of metal, such as those with metal scales, as determined by the GM. Likewise, the GM might determine some metal creatures are non-magnetic, depending on the metal they’re made of.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnetizing

</row>

---

This rune allows your armor to collapse into a disguised, portable form.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, interact;     **Effect** You doff your armor, which folds into another wearable object, such as a bangle or amulet with light Bulk. This wearable object has features that hint at the armor it hides. You aren’t wearing the armor while it’s in this form, but at the GM’s discretion, you can still activate properties that might feasibly come from the wearable item the armor has become. If the armor is in its portable form, you can use this activation to revert it to armor, which you can don in 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Spellbreaking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4370)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Counter-runes specific to a particular school of magic provide protections against that school. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and AC against one school of magic: [abjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=1), [conjuration](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=2), [divination](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=3), [enchantment](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=4), [evocation](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=5), [illusion](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=6), [necromancy](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=7), or [transmutation](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=8). The crafter chooses the school when creating the rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spellbreaking

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swallow-Spike](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4371)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Attacks made by armor with the _swallow-spike_ rune apply the Multiple Attack Penalty as if you had made them with another weapon._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your armor responds to your desire to break free of a creature grabbing you by growing spikes.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540))  **Trigger** You become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or otherwise held [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in a creature’s grasp, such as by being [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77);  **Effect** Your armor suddenly grows spikes, attacking the triggering creature. The armor makes a melee attack with an attack modifier of +14 that deals 2d6 piercing damage. If the creature is swallowing or engulfing you, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage, and damage from this attack can cut you free if it equals or exceeds the Rupture value of the immobilizing ability. This attack gets an item bonus to the attack roll equal to the armor’s item bonus to your AC and an item bonus to damage equal to double that amount.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540))   **Requirements** You’re being held [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) as described in the rune’s other activation; **Effect** Your armor attacks the creature immobilizing you. The armor makes a melee attack against the creature, as described in the rune’s other activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

**Price** 1,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The attack modifier increases to +22, the damage increases to 3d6, and the extra damage to an engulfing or swallowing creature increases to 2d6.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Swallow-Spike (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

**Price** 19,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The attack modifier increases to +28, the damage increases to 5d6, and the extra damage to an engulfing or swallowing creature increases to 3d6.
name: Swallow-Spike

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swallow-Spike](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4371)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 13

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Attacks made by armor with the _swallow-spike_ rune apply the Multiple Attack Penalty as if you had made them with another weapon._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your armor responds to your desire to break free of a creature grabbing you by growing spikes.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540))  **Trigger** You become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or otherwise held [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) in a creature’s grasp, such as by being [engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61) or [swallowed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77);  **Effect** Your armor suddenly grows spikes, attacking the triggering creature. The armor makes a melee attack with an attack modifier of +14 that deals 2d6 piercing damage. If the creature is swallowing or engulfing you, the attack deals an additional 1d6 damage, and damage from this attack can cut you free if it equals or exceeds the Rupture value of the immobilizing ability. This attack gets an item bonus to the attack roll equal to the armor’s item bonus to your AC and an item bonus to damage equal to double that amount.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540))   **Requirements** You’re being held [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) as described in the rune’s other activation; **Effect** Your armor attacks the creature immobilizing you. The armor makes a melee attack against the creature, as described in the rune’s other activation.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 14

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_ has four knobbed ribs that wrap around the torso. The armor grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 2 to poison damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ribs from the armor spread into functional spider limbs. For 1 minute, you gain a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) equal to half your land Speed and have your limbs free as you climb. If you use all your free limbs to help you [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), your climb Speed equals your land Speed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 7"

</traits>

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include four intact legs from an [ogre spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=814)

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) leather armor_, the resistance is 5, and the harness can be activated once every 10 minutes.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include four intact legs from a [goliath spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209)
name: Arachnid Harness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Autumn's Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4373)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 14

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leaf Weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Woven by fey seamstresses as rewards for servants of nature, countless leaves continually changing colors in autumnal hues comprise autumn’s embrace, a suit of _[+2 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [invisibility ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2790) [resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [leaf weave](/Armor.aspx?ID=28)_. Leaves shed from the armor as they might fall in autumn. When activating the armor’s _invisibility_ property rune, you disappear in a swirl of colorful leaves.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call forth a storm of leaves from _autumn’s embrace_. These leaves swirl in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) for 1 minute. Creatures within the area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and creatures outside the area are concealed to creatures within the leaves. However, you can see through this concealment. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Autumn's Embrace

</row>

---

Worn by [Iomedae’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) prestigious knights, this _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is emblazoned with the Inheritor’s religious symbol and sports a white cloak. While the cloak is white, this armor grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), provided your target has no enmity toward Iomedae.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;     **Effect** The armor’s cloak becomes red for 1 minute. As long as the cloak is red, you gain the benefits of the armor’s deflect melee trait without needing to spend an additional action to activate it during each turn.

**Craft Requirements** You worship Iomedae.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Hole Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4375)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 14

**Price** 8,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Fortress Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The joints of this black _[+2 greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789) [fortress plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=21)_ look like swirling vortices of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920). Non‑magical ammunition and thrown weapons aimed at you are destroyed after they hit you and deal damage or miss you. You also have resistance 10 to physical damage from ranged attacks. 

When an enemy’s ranged attack misses you or hits you and deals no damage, the armor absorbs the projectile. When it has absorbed six projectiles, the armor glows at the joints.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** A ranged weapon Strike targets a creature within 20 feet of you and the attacker hasn’t yet rolled its attack;  **Effect** The triggering [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) targets you instead of its intended target.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** The _black hole armor_ has absorbed six or more projectiles; **Effect** All the projectiles absorbed by the armor appear out of thin air, as though transported there, falling in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet of you. Each creature in the burst takes 10d8 piercing damage with a DC 35 Reflex save. The absorbed projectiles are all expended, and the armor’s joints stop glowing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Black Hole Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade Byrnie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4376)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 15

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Instead of chain links, this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43)_ is assembled from metal “leaves” that each resemble a small blade.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with it, or at the end of this turn if you don’t make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the [Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=4869) feat. 

Upgrading the runes on the blade byrnie makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a _+2 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+2 armor potency_ rune, or a _+3 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+3 armor potency_ rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blade Byrnie
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

---

Instead of chain links, this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43)_ is assembled from metal “leaves” that each resemble a small blade.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You pull a link from the armor, which transforms into a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_. The dagger disappears and reappears as a link in the armor after you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with it, or at the end of this turn if you don’t make a Strike. You can Activate the blade byrnie in place of an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to draw a weapon for abilities such as the [Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=4869) feat. 

Upgrading the runes on the blade byrnie makes the daggers pulled from it more powerful. The daggers have a _+2 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+2 armor potency_ rune, or a _+3 weapon potency_ rune if the armor has a _+3 armor potency_ rune.

    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devil's Bargain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4377)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 15

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Those favored by [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278) can be found wearing this _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)_, which is lacquered in red and black.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically fail an attack roll, check, or saving throw;  **Effect** You offer a prayer to Asmodeus, who accepts under a reciprocal condition. Reroll the triggering roll with a +2 circumstance bonus and use the higher result.

If this reroll improves your degree of success, the GM can later reroll one Strike against you, one saving throw against your abilities, or one secret check or saving throw you make. This is a fortune effect if used on a Strike or save against you, or a misfortune effect if used on your secret check or saving throw. The GM must use this reroll before the end of the last day you activated the armor.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devil's Bargain

This harness of _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49)_ is made from the shell plates of a [dragon turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2956) and has the [aquadynamic](/Traits.aspx?ID=792) trait. Fashioned from organic material, the suit is one of the few heavy armors not anathema to [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack;  **Effect** You roll the hefty plates of the dragon turtle armor toward the attacker to gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the shell of a [dragon turtle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2956).

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

---

This suit of _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789) [lattice armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=54)_ has latticework of fine golden wire. After negating a critical hit with its fortification rune, the latticework glows for 1 minute, shedding [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet). You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this light.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610))   **Requirements** The _energizing lattice_ is glowing because it negated an enemy’s critical hit; **Effect** You release the lattice’s energy and make a weapon or unarmed attack Strike that deals 6d6 additional force damage. If your Strike fails, but doesn’t critically fail, the target still takes half the force damage. The latticework ceases glowing after the Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Lattice

>
[Immortal Bastion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4380)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 15

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 5
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Bastion Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This impressive _[+3 greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [greater fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789) [bastion plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=17)_ is built like an impregnable castle, with multiple layers of defense and no weak points. When you activate the armor’s deflect melee trait, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against melee attacks instead of +1, and you gain 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are reduced to 0 Hit Points or would die from a [death effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331);  **Effect** You drop to 1 Hit Point instead of being reduced to 0 HP or dying, and you gain 100 temporary Hit Points that last until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would gain or increase the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition;  **Effect** You avoid gaining or increasing the condition. If the triggering effect imposes both doomed and wounded, choose only one to prevent. This doesn’t remove either of the conditions if you already have them, nor does it prevent the same triggering effect from giving or increasing the prevented condition later.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Immortal Bastion

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Linnorm's Sankeit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4382)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 18

**Price** 22,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=53)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The first suit of _[+3 greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [antimagic ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2787) [sankeit](/Armor.aspx?ID=53)_ made in this fashion was crafted for the Varki linnorm king Nankou, after he claimed his title by slaying a [taiga linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=723). By Varki tradition, the armor was crafted using some of the slain linnorm’s body to decorate the breastplate and helm, which imbued the armor with several of the linnorm’s natural abilities. Though the helm is shaped like the beast’s head, a linnorm’s actual head would be too large for a proper helmet.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You breathe a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of electrified vapor, dealing 17d6 electricity damage to creatures in the area (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The electrified mist persists in the area for 2 rounds, dealing 5d6 electricity damage (DC 38 basic Reflex save) to each creature that ends its turn in the mist. If you slew the linnorm this armor is made from, you can use the higher of your class DC or spell DC instead of the listed DCs for this effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** A creature adjacent to you targets you with a melee attack;  **Effect** The creature takes 4d6 piercing damage as previously invisible magical spines leap outwards from the armor to punish them for the attack.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide and skull of a [taiga linnorm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=723)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Linnorm's Sankeit

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ouroboros Buckles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4383)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 18

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Buckle Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=18)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ominous-looking _[+3 greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [greater acid‑resistant ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2788) [buckle armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=18)_ is deep red in color, favored by assassins who worship [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=289) or [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), and if unfastened, the many belts and buckles writhe like living snakes. _Ouroboros buckles_ have the [comfort](/Traits.aspx?ID=556) trait. However, while wearing _ouroboros buckles_, the unfathomable concept of infinity pulls at your mind, and each time you awaken, you’d swear the armor hissed into your ear while you were asleep. The hissing suggested secret wisdom to you in [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), though you only ever remember it vaguely, like a fading dream.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call forth the cursed regenerative blood of an ouroboros to recover from your wounds. You gain [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 15 for 1 minute; fire damage deactivates this regeneration. Each time you regain Hit Points from this regeneration, if you’re in your normal form, you collapse into a Large swarm of Tiny snakes. This is a [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect that changes you into a battle form.

When you’re a swarm of snakes, you gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411), imprecise scent (30 feet), a Speed of 20 feet, a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 20 feet, and a swim Speed of 20 feet. You have an AC of 16 + your level and ignore your armor’s check penalty and Speed reduction. Also, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to physical damage and [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to [area](/Rules.aspx?ID=2384) damage and splash damage. You can use none of your normal Strikes. Instead, you can use Swarming Bites, a single action that deals 4d4 piercing damage plus 2d6 poison damage to creatures in your space with a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

If you’re in this battle form and are already at full HP when your regeneration occurs, the snakes instantaneously slither together, and you transform into your original form.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** A foe within 15 feet hits you with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that deals slashing or piercing damage;  **Effect** Your _ouroboros buckles_ spray acidic blood in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) toward the foe, dealing 5d6 acid damage with a DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include blood from an [ouroboros](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1256) to tan the leather.

</column>
name: Ouroboros Buckles

Legends speak of a variety of similar coral armors with distinct powers, but surface-dwellers know of reef heart as a magic armor that makes it easier for them to travel under the sea. Reef heart enables you to breathe underwater and gives you a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) equal to half your land Speed.

If you conjure the coral underwater, it ceases dealing damage after 1 minute, but its duration is unlimited. If the magical coral remains in place for 1 year, it becomes non-magical coral from which a reef might grow.

[Wasp Guard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4388)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 19

**Price** 487 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

[Druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) and [Calistria’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=279) faithful alike value this vindictive armor for its ability to turn away pests and exact revenge on foes in a manner that can easily turn coordinated enemies into a chaotic mass. Wasp guard is _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)_ with the carapaces of [wasps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=483), subtly diverting swarms away and granting a +1 status bonus to Reflex saving throws against Swarming Bites, Swarming Stings, and similar abilities from swarms.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy within 30 feet [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) you with an attack;  **Effect** You animate the exoskeletons of the _wasp guard_ armor into a spectral swarm of wasps that seeks revenge for the slight. The swarm flies to the triggering enemy’s space and swarms about them, dealing 1d8 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). If the affected enemy has any allies within 30 feet, the enemy can spend a single action, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to shoo the swarm away. This ends the persistent damage on that enemy automatically, but the swarm instead moves to affect the enemy’s ally.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1 Bulk of wasp exoskeletons.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wasp Guard

---

.[Barbarians](/Classes.aspx?ID=57), [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) and other outlanders are often forced to spend the harsh winter months protecting their communities from one of the deadliest predators to stalk the forests and taiga of the northern reaches, the fearsome [winter wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=403). This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)_ is assembled from the hide and fur of a trio of winter wolves, and it conveys on the wearer both an attack akin to the winter wolves' deadly jaws and the ability to channel the frigid cold of the winter wolf's Breath Weapon.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You breathe a cloud of frost in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that deals 5d8 cold damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain a jaws unarmed attack that you make using your hands. This attack deals 1d8 piercing damage plus 1 cold damage, is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and has the [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716) and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hides of at least three winter wolves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wolfjaw Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helmsman's Recourse](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4391)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Meteor Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This standard-grade darkwood [meteor shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=13) (Hardness 7, HP 28, BT 14) is a wheel from a ship. While wielding the shield, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to Swim.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re in a body of water; **Effect** For 10 minutes, you don’t sink if you haven’t succeeded at a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) action on a turn, and if you’re submerged, you automatically ascend 10 feet at the end of your turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 8, HP 60, and BT 30, and when you Activate the shield, it casts [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) on you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helmsman's Recourse (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 2,650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 11, HP 80, and BT 40; the item bonus is +2; and when you Activate the shield, you can breathe underwater for 10 minutes.
name: Helmsman's Recourse

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sapling Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4393)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17), [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This surprisingly weighty, though well-balanced, wooden buckler is emblazoned with the image of a sapling. The sapling withers as the shield takes damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** The buckler expands, with the sapling image growing into a mighty oak tree. The buckler becomes a [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20), gaining the corresponding AC bonus, Speed penalty, and ability to [Take Cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2556). It keeps the same Hit Points and Broken Threshold, but its Hardness and Bulk increase by 2 in this form. The shield remains in this form until you Activate it again to revert it to a buckler.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 57 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The buckler has Hardness 3, HP 24, and BT 12.

right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

</traits>

This surprisingly weighty, though well-balanced, wooden buckler is emblazoned with the image of a sapling. The sapling withers as the shield takes damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** The buckler expands, with the sapling image growing into a mighty oak tree. The buckler becomes a [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20), gaining the corresponding AC bonus, Speed penalty, and ability to [Take Cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2556). It keeps the same Hit Points and Broken Threshold, but its Hardness and Bulk increase by 2 in this form. The shield remains in this form until you Activate it again to revert it to a buckler.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Starfall Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4394)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 23,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Heavy Rondache](/Shields.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This high-grade [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) [heavy rondache](/Shields.aspx?ID=10) (Hardness 17, HP 100, BT 50) bears a _[+3 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=396)_ and has dents that resemble craters on its surface. While wielding the shield, you have fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 15.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to three times. This movement doesn’t trigger reactions and ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). At the end of your movement, you deal 6d10 bludgeoning damage in a 10-foot emanation and 14d6 fire damage in a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save, with the results applying to both the bludgeoning and fire damage). The space where you end your movement and all adjacent spaces become difficult terrain for 1 minute, and the shield glows red-hot for 10 minutes, during which it gains the effect of the _[greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838)_ property rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Starfall Shield

**Base Shield** [Salvo Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=15)

</row>

---

The face of this standard-grade [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) [salvo shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=15) (Hardness 7, HP 28, BT 14) is polished to a mirror finish.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** A ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) using ammunition such as arrows, bolts, or bullets (but not siege rounds or larger projectiles) misses you; **Requirements** You have the _turnabout shield_ raised; **Effect** The ammunition enters the shield and is redirected with the same force with which it was originally fired. Make a ranged Strike using the ammunition with an attack modifier of +19, targeting a creature within 60 feet.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warding Escutcheon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4396)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 23

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Tower Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An image of a keyhole adorns the coat of arms of this stone [tower shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=20) (Hardness 10, HP 40, BT 20).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield becomes a locked door in an empty, adjacent space no wider than 5 feet, becoming flush with the surrounding structure so it blocks all passage. The door has the same Hardness, HP, and Broken Threshold as the shield, with [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DCs of 25 to open. The door remains in place for 1 hour, until it’s opened or [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), or until you spend an action to end this effect, at which point the shield returns to your hand (or falls to the ground in your space if your hands are full).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield floats lazily in the air as bricks quickly fly from it to form a squat stone tower around it. You cast [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), with the shield becoming the door.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Escutcheon
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Warding Escutcheon (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 22

**Price** 55,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 20, HP 80, and BT 40. The DCs are 43, and using the second activation casts [_resplendent mansion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2018), forming a tall stone keep.
name: Warding Escutcheon

The door remains in place for 1 hour, until it’s opened or [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), or until you spend an action to end this effect, at which point the shield returns to your hand (or falls to the ground in your space if your hands are full).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield floats lazily in the air as bricks quickly fly from it to form a squat stone tower around it. You cast [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), with the shield becoming the door.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Authorized](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4397)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 32

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp needles impale anyone who attempts to wield this weapon other than its rightful owner. Each _authorized_ rune is etched with the blood of a specific creature. If any other creature wields the weapon, needles immediately erupt from the weapon’s hilt or shaft, dealing 1d8 piercing damage plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to the wielder. If the weapon has a [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) rune, this damage increases to 1d8 per damage die and 1d4 persistent damage per damage die; this counts only the weapon’s base die and dice from the striking rune. The persistent bleed damage can’t end while the creature still holds the weapon. The spikes retract once the creature lets go.

When the rune is crafted, the crafter can choose to broaden the criteria for who can safely wield the item, expanding the users to creatures with a particular alignment, bloodline, or patron deity, as the crafter chooses. This criterion must be shared by the creature who contributed the blood for the rune.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Authorized

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When etched, this rune creates an extradimensional space that links to the weapon that wields it. The space can hold up to 1 Bulk but can contain only poisons and magic oils that could be applied to the weapon. Stowing or retrieving an item in the space requires an Interact action, except when using the rune’s activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;    **Requirements** At least one magic oil or poison is stored inside the rune’s extradimensional space; **Effect** For 1 minute, you can apply stored oils and poisons to the weapon without needing any hands free. Applying them takes the same number of actions as normal. An oil or poison applied this way pours directly from the extradimensional space onto the weapon, and when it’s fully applied, its empty vial is ejected.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Coating

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The weapon casts a [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687) spell. Use your normal attack bonus and damage for the weapon instead of the damage listed in the spell, but use your Wisdom modifier instead of Strength when determining damage. You can choose to make a Flurry of Blows instead of a Strike when the _spiritual weapon_ attacks; this still counts as your flourish for the turn. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation in the same manner as the spell.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You and the weapon flash to a perfect attacking position, then return to where you started. On this Strike, ignore any circumstance penalty, status penalty, and range increment penalty.

Merciful weapons are sheathed in an unmistakable wispy green aura recognized by both gladiators and guards around the world. A merciful weapon has the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait and can’t be used to make a lethal attack. Any [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) the weapon would deal is negated.

On a critical hit, a merciful weapon causes the target to become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the weapon’s wielder for 1 minute, as the target is bombarded with feelings of guilt and remorse. This is a mental effect. If you have access to the weapon’s critical specialization effect, you choose whether to use it or the merciful effect.

You use the bolas to attempt a ranged [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) against all creatures in a 5-foot radius burst within the weapon’s first range increment. Roll a separate [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check for each target. Each attempt counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but don’t increase your penalty until you have made all the attempts.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 35 **Price** 4,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403) --- The Celestial word for “repent” is etched in golden lettering on the shaft of this _[+2 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [merciful ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1869) [mithral ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403)_. An [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)-aligned creature is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 when carrying or wielding this weapon. **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You hold your weapon aloft, calling on righteousness to steel your resolve. You cast [_protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1641) on yourself and you must select [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) as the chosen alignment. **Redeemer Wielder** If you’re a [champion](/Classes.aspx?ID=58) of the [redeemer](/Causes.aspx?ID=14) cause, you also gain the following two benefits.
  • When you critically hit an evil creature with the blessed reformer, until the end of your next turn, the target takes a –10-foot penalty to its Speeds and can’t [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304).
  • You can Activate the weapon in the following way.
**Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** You hit a creature using the _blessed reformer_ as your last action; **Effect** You cast [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) at the same level as your [champion](/Classes.aspx?ID=58) focus spells. It must target the creature you hit, and the creature takes a status penalty on its save against the spell equal to the _blessed reformer’s_ number of weapon damage dice. **Craft Requirements** You’re a champion with the redeemer cause. The initial materials must include 140 gp of [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2799).

This fleshy _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404)_ is obviously crafted from the tentacle of some fearsome beast, likely an [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358), and constantly drips slime.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The next creature you successfully Strike with this weapon is exposed to alghollthu slime.

**Alghollthu Slime** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Stage 1** no ill effect (1 round); **Stage 2** the victim’s skin softens, making the creature [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** the victim’s skin transforms into a clear, slimy membrane, making it drained 2 until the curse ends; every hour this membrane remains dry, the creature’s drained condition increases by 1 (permanent). A [_remove disease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) spell can [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) this curse, but immunity to disease offers no protection against it.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a tentacle from an [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358).

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"

>
[Alicorn Lance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4415)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The version of the _alicorn lance_ which does evil damage may not be purchased or crafted in Pathfinder Society play._

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=383)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This white _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=383)_ is made from the horn of a [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504), willingly granted at the end of its lifetime. In addition to its normal damage, it deals 1 [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) damage on successful attacks. You can make the lance glow like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760) or suppress its light by using an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You evoke the spirit of the unicorn that donated the _alicorn lance’s_ horn, which you ride in a shining charge. Move up to twice your Speed and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the alicorn lance; you gain the effects of the lance’s [jousting](/Traits.aspx?ID=638) trait on this Strike. If you were already mounted when you Activate the horn, the unicorn spirit takes shape around your steed, granting it a +10–foot status bonus to its Speed for the charge.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a horn willingly gifted by a [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504). In rare instances, an _alicorn lance_ can be made with a forcibly taken horn. However this heinous act pollutes the horn’s magic, causing it to glow with a sickly red light, deal [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64) damage instead of [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) damage, and make the unicorn spirit evoked in the charge to whinny visibly in pain.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alicorn Lance

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dezullon Fountain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4416)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=125" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Air Repeater](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _dezullon fountain_ is a distinct type of _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [air repeater](/Weapons.aspx?ID=188)_ made from the still-living pitcher of a [dezullon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2912), dealing acid damage instead of the gun’s normal piercing damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The next creature you successfully Strike with this weapon is exposed to amnesia venom.

**Amnesia Venom** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) (1 round); **Stage 2** flat-footed and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), flat-footed, and clumsy 2 (1 round)

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a pitcher from a [dezullon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2912) or similar creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dezullon Fountain

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonscale Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4417)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

**Price** 240 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Bo Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_ is covered in scales shed by or harvested from a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395). When benefiting from the circumstance bonus to AC granted by the bo staff’s [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait, you also gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), according to the type of damage dealt by the breath weapon of the dragon that provided the scales; for instance, a staff made with scales taken from a [white dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=647) would provide cold resistance. When this resistance prevents you from taking damage, the _dragonscale staff_ deals an additional 1 damage of the type matching its resistance for 1 round.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include scales from a dragon with a breath weapon that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonscale Staff

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragontooth Club](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4418)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 36

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Leiomano](/Weapons.aspx?ID=292)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dragon teeth line the edges of this _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [leiomano](/Weapons.aspx?ID=292)_. The leiomano also has a [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), or [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834) rune, as appropriate for the dragon from which the teeth were taken, which also grants the weapon the associated energy trait (for instance, fire for a club made with teeth taken from a [red dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=582)).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You swing the leiomano, sending several of the dragon teeth shooting through the air on jets of energy. The dragon teeth deal 3d6 piercing damage and 3d6 damage of the energy type corresponding to the weapon in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The teeth hunt down their targets, correcting their flight in midair, which reduces any circumstance bonus from [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) by 2.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include teeth from a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395) with a breath weapon that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragontooth Club
